body.title: CLOSED: NE WATCH_Q→WATCH_T context injection
body.result: WATCH_Q then WATCH_T context injected into NE _build_prompt via new _watch_context() method
body.description:
In narrative_engine.py context assembly, ensure WATCH_Q_* nodes (what does this mean) are loaded and injected before WATCH_T_* nodes (what does this mean to me). This implements two-step latent variable compression: broad semantic matching collapses to personal relevance in order — matching the neuroscience paper's 'ramping to threshold' pattern and the existing WATCH_Q/WATCH_T tree structure in clan.memories.

The change is ordering only — no new queries, no new memory types. Add an explicit comment at the join point naming the WATCH_Q → WATCH_T sequence so future readers understand the ordering is intentional.

Note: narrative_engine.py is touched by the already-filed T-igor-playbook-memory-type (same decision batch D-dreaming-patterns-2026-05-10); this is consistent with that precedent.

**Affected files:** `wild_igor/igor/cognition/narrative_engine.py` (context block ordering — no new queries, no new memory types)
**Design rules:** no cortex.py changes; WATCH_Q → WATCH_T ordering explicit with comment at join point; budget-aware — same token caps as existing context blocks apply; HIGH-inertia narrative_engine.py touch is ordering-only (consistent with T-igor-playbook-memory-type precedent)
**Scope boundary:** IN — context assembly order change, WATCH_Q before WATCH_T, explicit ordering comment. OUT — no new DB queries, no new memory types, no activate() calls (T-igor-activate-primitive), no scoring changes.
**Test plan:** NE prompt construction test: given fixture with both WATCH_Q_test and WATCH_T_test nodes, WATCH_Q block appears before WATCH_T block in assembled prompt string; existing NE context assembly tests still pass
